Title: CBF4/DREB1D represses XERICO to attenuate ABA, osmotic and drought stress responses in Arabidopsis

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: xer-1 is a hypomorphic mutant with a 5'-UTR T-DNA insertion leading to reduced XER expression, whereas xer-2 is a CRISPR/Cas9-generated null allele with deletion of most of the coding region. In xer-2 cbf4-2 double mutants, the xer-2 phenotype is epistatic to cbf4-2 in ABA sensitivity, stomatal development and drought/osmotic stress responses, placing XER downstream of CBF4.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. XER is expressed in the epidermis of young Arabidopsis seedlings, including all stomatal lineage cells, and its transcript levels are induced by osmotic stress (mannitol).
2. Loss-of-function xer-1 and xer-2 mutants show reduced ABA sensitivity in seedling assays, increased stomatal density and stomatal index, and impaired ABA- and mannitol-induced stomatal closure.
3. xer mutants are more susceptible to drought, exhibiting higher percentages of wilted rosette tissue after water deprivation and faster water loss and higher transpiration/stomatal conductance than wild type.
4. Genetic analyses show that xer-2 is epistatic to cbf4-2 in ABA, osmotic and drought responses and stomatal development, indicating that XER acts downstream of CBF4 to promote ABA-dependent stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: XER inhibits stomatal development and promotes ABA sensitivity and drought/osmotic stress tolerance; loss-of-function xer mutants have higher stomatal density and stomatal index, reduced ABA sensitivity during seedling growth, impaired ABA- and mannitol-induced stomatal closure, and increased susceptibility to drought with greater wilted tissue and water loss.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: XER functions as a RING E3 ubiquitin ligase that modulates ABA levels, and its transcription is negatively regulated by the transcription factor CBF4, which binds the DRE element in the XER 5'-UTR and represses promoter activity and endogenous expression, especially under osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in the ABA-dependent osmotic and drought stress response pathway to promote ABA accumulation, stomatal closure and reduced stomatal density, contributing to water-deficit tolerance; functions downstream of CBF4 in this regulatory pathway.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ABA metabolism and ABA signaling pathway involved in osmotic and drought stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: CBF4 --(direct binding to DRE element in 5'-UTR, transcriptional repression)--> XER.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: XER is expressed in young seedlings, with XERpro:YFP activity in cotyledons and hypocotyls of 3- and 5-day-old seedlings, including pavement cells, meristemoids and mature guard cells in the cotyledon epidermis; XER transcripts are induced in 5-day-old seedlings by 300 mM mannitol, with early upregulation followed by downregulation as CBF4 accumulates.
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: xer-1: T-DNA insertion in the XER 5'-UTR (promoter/5'UTR region, affects_expression and affects_function, hypomorphic allele). xer-2: CRISPR/Cas9-induced deletion removing most of the XER coding region (genic, affects_function, null allele). Both alleles reduce XER transcript levels and impair ABA and drought stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA (1 μM for seedling growth assays), mannitol (300 mM for osmotic stress), and soil water deprivation for 10–12 days followed by rewatering (drought stress).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: ABA-mediated osmotic and drought stress responses and stomatal development/opening in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and osmotic stress), ABA sensitivity, stomatal density and stomatal conductance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: xer-1 and xer-2 mutants display significantly higher stomatal indices and stomatal densities on the abaxial cotyledon epidermis than Col-0; xer mutants are significantly less sensitive to 1 μM ABA in cotyledon expansion assays; after 10 days of water deprivation and 24 h rewatering, xer plants show a significantly higher percentage of wilted rosette leaf tissue than wild type and greater cumulative water loss from detached leaves.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: xer mutants show reduced numbers of pavement cells while total epidermal cell number is unchanged; xer-2 and xer-2 cbf4-2 leaves lose water faster than wild type in detached leaf assays and have moderately higher transpiration rate and stomatal conductance than Col-0.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 wild type; xer-1 hypomorphic T-DNA mutant (SALK_075188) with a 5'-UTR insertion; xer-2 CRISPR/Cas9-induced null mutant lacking most of the XER coding region; XERpro:YFP reporter lines; xer-2 cbf4-2 double mutant for epistasis analysis.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis and T-DNA mutant analysis; PCR genotyping; qRT-PCR for XER transcript levels; ABA sensitivity assays based on cotyledon expansion; water deprivation and rewatering survival assays; detached leaf water loss and gas exchange; stomatal density and stomatal index measurements on toluidine blue-stained cotyledons; stomatal aperture assays under ABA and mannitol; confocal microscopy of XERpro:YFP; time-course expression analysis under mannitol.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics using an existing T-DNA hypomorphic mutant xer-1 (SALK_075188) with a 5'-UTR insertion and a CRISPR/Cas9-generated null allele xer-2 deleting most of the coding region; XERpro:YFP transcriptional fusion used to analyze expression.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: XER promotes ABA accumulation, stomatal closure and drought/osmotic stress tolerance, and restricts stomatal development. Enhancing XER activity or expression in crops could reduce stomatal density and promote ABA-induced stomatal closure, thereby decreasing transpiration and improving drought and osmotic stress tolerance and water-use efficiency, although excessive ABA accumulation and growth costs would need to be balanced.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: CBF4 belongs to the CBF/DREB1 subfamily of AP2/ERF transcription factors and contains an AP2 DNA-binding domain and a partial EAR repression motif. The cbf4 null mutants exhibit increased ABA sensitivity and drought resistance similar to CBF4 overexpression lines reported previously, suggesting that ectopic overexpression may cause dominant-negative effects through misregulation of interacting complexes or off-target binding.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. CBF4 is weakly expressed under normal conditions but is strongly induced by ABA, mannitol and dehydration in Arabidopsis seedlings and adult plants, with ABA-inducible expression in cotyledon and hypocotyl epidermis including stomatal lineage cells.
2. CBF4 localizes to the nucleus and directly binds the DRE/CRT element in the XER 5'-UTR, associating with the XER promoter in Y1H and DAP-seq and repressing XER promoter-driven luciferase and endogenous XER transcripts; repression is abolished when the DRE is mutated.
3. cbf4 loss-of-function mutants are hypersensitive to ABA during seedling growth, more tolerant to drought with reduced wilted tissue, lower water loss, and lower transpiration and stomatal conductance than wild type.
4. cbf4 mutants have reduced stomatal index and density, and their stomata close more in response to ABA and mannitol; in xer-2 cbf4-2 double mutants, xer-2 is epistatic, demonstrating that CBF4 acts upstream of XER as a transcriptional repressor that dampens ABA and stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CBF4 is a negative regulator of ABA and drought/osmotic stress responses and promotes stomatal development and stomatal opening; cbf4 null mutants are hypersensitive to ABA during seedling growth, more tolerant to drought with reduced wilting and water loss, have reduced stomatal index and density, and exhibit hypersensitive ABA- and mannitol-induced stomatal closure.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: CBF4 is induced by ABA and osmotic stress, localizes to the nucleus, directly binds the DRE/CRT element in the XER 5'-UTR, and represses XER promoter activity and endogenous XER expression. This repression is lost when the core DRE nucleotides are mutated, and inducible expression of CBF4 blocks XER upregulation under mannitol, indicating direct transcriptional repression of XER by CBF4.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in ABA-dependent osmotic and drought stress signaling as a negative regulator that attenuates stress responses. CBF4 is upregulated by ABA and osmotic stress and acts as a transcriptional repressor of XER via the DRE element in its 5'-UTR, thereby damping XER-mediated ABA accumulation, stomatal closure and drought tolerance; genetic analysis places CBF4 upstream of XER in this pathway.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: CBF4 --(direct binding to DRE/CRT element in 5'-UTR, transcriptional repression)--> XER.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CBF/DREB1 transcription factor of the AP2/ERF family, containing an AP2 DNA-binding domain and a partial EAR (LxL) repression motif.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, as shown by 35Spro:CBF4-YFP localization to nuclei in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf epidermal cells and in hypocotyl cells of 5-day-old Arabidopsis seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: CBF4 is expressed at very low levels under normal conditions but is transcriptionally upregulated by ABA and osmotic stress (300 mM mannitol) in 5-day-old seedlings and by dehydration in seedlings and adult plants. CBF4pro:YFP shows ABA-induced expression in the cotyledon and hypocotyl epidermis, including stomatal lineage cells.
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Three independent cbf4 null alleles generated by CRISPR/Cas9 carry large deletions within the CBF4 coding region, including removal of the AP2 DNA-binding domain (genic, affects_function), resulting in loss of CBF4 function and altered ABA and drought/osmotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: CRISPR/Cas9-induced deletions (indels) in the coding region.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment (50–100 μM for expression and 1 μM for seedling sensitivity assays), mannitol (300 mM for osmotic stress), dehydration, and soil water deprivation (drought) followed by rewatering.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Transcriptional regulation of ABA, osmotic and drought stress responses and stomatal development through CBF4-mediated repression of XER in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and osmotic stress), ABA sensitivity, stomatal density and stomatal aperture.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: All cbf4 deletion mutants display significantly increased sensitivity to 1 μM ABA, with reduced cotyledon expansion compared with Col-0; cbf4 plants have a significantly lower percentage of wilted rosette leaf tissue than wild type after 10 days of water deprivation and 24 h rewatering; detached cbf4 leaves lose less water over 5 h than wild type and show reduced transpiration rate and stomatal conductance; cbf4-2 seedlings have significantly lower stomatal index and stomatal density than wild type, and their stomata show a significantly greater reduction in aperture index in response to 300 mM mannitol or 10 μM ABA.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: cbf4-2 plants show lower transpiration rate and stomatal conductance than wild type in detached leaf gas-exchange measurements; cbf4-2 has reduced stomatal index and fewer stomata on cotyledons, and its stomata close more in response to ABA and mannitol compared with Col-0; in xer-2 cbf4-2 double mutants, xer-2 is epistatic, indicating CBF4 acts upstream of XER.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 wild type; three independent cbf4 CRISPR/Cas9 deletion mutants lacking different portions of the coding region including the AP2 domain (e.g. cbf4-2); CBF4pro:YFP reporter lines; 35Spro:CBF4-YFP overexpression lines; DEX-inducible CBF4 (iCBF4) lines; xer-2 cbf4-2 double mutant; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves transiently expressing CBF4-YFP or XER 5'UTR:fLUC with or without 35Spro:CBF4 effector.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Yeast one-hybrid screening and one-on-one Y1H confirmation; analysis of public DAP-seq and high-throughput Y1H data; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis and PCR genotyping of cbf4 mutants; qRT-PCR of CBF4 and XER under ABA and mannitol treatments and in DEX-inducible iCBF4 lines; promoter:YFP confocal imaging; subcellular localization of 35Spro:CBF4-YFP in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana; ABA sensitivity assays (cotyledon expansion); water deprivation and recovery assays; detached leaf water-loss and gas-exchange measurements; stomatal index, stomatal density and stomatal aperture assays; dual-luciferase reporter assays testing CBF4 repression of XER 5'UTR-driven luciferase and DRE-mutated reporter.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as an upstream regulator of XER in a yeast one-hybrid screen using the XER 5'-UTR (−1 to −630 bp) as bait against a library of ~1500 full-length Arabidopsis transcription factors; functional analysis performed using CRISPR/Cas9-generated cbf4 deletion mutants, CBF4pro:YFP transcriptional reporter lines, 35Spro:CBF4-YFP overexpression lines and a DEX-inducible CBF4 (iCBF4) construct.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: CBF4 functions as a stress-induced transcriptional repressor that dampens ABA and osmotic/drought stress responses by repressing XER and likely other positive regulators of ABA biosynthesis and signaling. Loss-of-function of CBF4 increases ABA sensitivity and drought tolerance but reduces stomatal index. Modulating CBF4 activity or expression, for example by reducing its function in a controlled manner, could enhance drought tolerance and water-use efficiency via stronger ABA-mediated responses, although its role in buffering stress responses to sustain growth means that breeding applications must carefully balance enhanced tolerance with potential impacts on growth and stomatal development.
